tor.TypedAtomicActor ptolemy.actor.lib.x10.X10Interface All Implemented Interfaces: Actor , Changeable , java.lang.Cloneable, Debuggable , DebugListener , Derivable , Executable , Instantiable , ModelErrorHandler , MoMLExportable , Nameable , java.io

ErrorHandler , MoMLExportable , Nameable , java.io.Serializable, TypedActor Direct Known Subclasses: Receiver , Sender public class X10Interface extends TypedAtomicActor This class abstracts X10-interface devices for X10 communication via the seri

face devices for X10 communication via the serial port. Currently, this class supports the following X10 serial port models for communication with a host computer: "CM11A" serial module (wired) "CM17A" serial module (wireless) Note that the wir

"CM17A" serial module (wireless) Note that the wireless serial module is unidirectional, only sending commands to the X10 network, and not receiving from it. Thus, the Listener, CommandSensor, and LevelSensor actors will not work with it. F

and LevelSensor actors will not work with it. For instructions concerning the physical setup of these devices, refer to their respective manuals. This actor requires that the Java comm API be installed. The comm API comes from http://java.su

stalled. The comm API comes from http://java.sun.com/products/javacomm/ To install the comm API on a Windows machine: place the win32com.dll in $JDK\jre\bin directory. make sure the win32com.dll is executable. Place the comm.jar in $JDK\jre\lib\ex

executable. Place the comm.jar in $JDK\jre\lib\ext. Place the javax.comm.properties in $JDK\jre\lib . where $JDK is the location of your Java development kit. If the x10Interface or serialPortName parameters are changed after preinitialize() is c

rameters are changed after preinitialize() is called, the changes will not take effect until the next execution of the model. Derived classes must implement send and receive functionality. Furthermore, this class requires the x10 library (ja

rthermore, this class requires the x10 library (jar), which can be obtained from http://x10.homelinux.org/download.html . Unzip the file tjx10p-11.zip (Version 1.1) and install it in $PTII/vendors/misc/x10. Then re-run configure in $PTII and

ndors/misc/x10. Then re-run configure in $PTII and rebuild to compile the actors in the x10 library. The configure script looks for the library in tjx10p-11/lib/x10.jar. After installing the serial communication and X10 packages, rerun confi

ial communication and X10 packages, rerun configure: cd $PTII ./configure Since: Ptolemy II 4.0 Version: $Id: X10Interface.java,v 1.8.2.1 2004/06/18 00:48:58 cxh Exp $ Author: Colin Cochran and Edward A. Lee See Also: Serialized Form Accepted R

a string with default "CM11A". Currently, options are: CM11A CM17A serialPortName public StringParameter serialPortName Attribute giving the serial port to use. This is a string with the default being the first serial port listed by the javax.

being the first serial port listed by the javax.comm.CommPortIdentifier class. If there are no serial ports available (meaning probably that the javax.comm package is not installed properly), then the value of the string will be "no ports av
